ops.text: E30.5 UPDATE: SANITY GATE PASSED exactly (recomputed AUC 0.9316896744944222 = target, abs_diff 0.0, frame digest e85dc4a2, 335/293 — eval-what-you-serve harness confirmed faithful, $0). But train_score.py died on seed 1 with a Tinker TLS error: `invalid peer certificate: UnknownIssuer` at the auth_token endpoint, via pyqwest (Tinker's Rust httpx transport). Diagnosis so far: curl to https://tinker.thinkingmachines.dev validates fine (http 302, ssl_verify=0) using the system store + SSL_CERT_FILE=/etc/ssl/cert.pem; api_key resolves (error is post-ServiceClient-construction); the detached run_all.sh did NOT `eval "$(ccp env)"`. So it's a cert-trust config gap in the FRESH experiment venv's pyqwest (not transient, not Tinker-down, not $ — $0 spent). Same Tinker path feeds E37 + E30.2a cross-fit retrains, so fixing pyqwest cert trust in fresh experiment venvs is shared infra. Delegated the diagnosis+fix; run_all.sh will relaunch detached with the fix (resumable — seed 1 retries, sanity.json already passed). Also noted: train_score.py aborts the whole run on first-seed failure instead of retrying transient Tinker errors — the fix should make it retry per athome's own retry contract.
